tldr: Headlines across nine country buckets report in near-uniform terms that a U.S. judge ordered Donald Trump to pay E. Jean Carroll roughly $5–6.5 million following the Supreme Court's refusal to hear his appeal, framing the story as the culmination of a sexual-abuse and defamation verdict; Canadian feeds additionally carry a separate story on Trump's $2.2 billion in personal earnings during his first year back in office.

evidence.quote: E. Jean Carroll to be paid $5-million
Judge orders $5.8M be paid to E. Jean Carroll
Judge's order after Supreme Court setback explained
after series of court losses in sex-abuse case
Judge orders Trump to pay writer $5 mn for assault
'It is time': Donald Trump loses bid to delay
mujer que acusó a Donald Trump de agresión sexual
Trump pague US$ 5,8 milhões a escritora por abuso sexual
agresión sexual y difamación a la escritora E. Jean Carroll
jury found Trump sexually abused and defamed her
be paid to E. Jean Carroll in Trump sex abuse case
How Trump made $2.2B US in his 1st year back in office

single_outlet_findings.finding: Only outlet to report $6.5 million rather than $5–5.8 million, likely incorporating accrued interest into the headline figure — a discrepancy absent from every other international title and potentially misleading about the base jury award amount.

Only outlet to bundle a wholly separate story — Trump's $2.2 billion in personal earnings during his first year in office — alongside the Carroll judgment, producing a compound portrait of presidential wealth and legal liability unique to the Canadian public-broadcaster feed.

bottom_line: The headline layer presents near-global consensus that a court has forced Trump to pay Carroll for sexual abuse and defamation, with the Supreme Court's refusal to hear his appeal making the judgment final, while Canada's feeds uniquely add a wealth-framed sidebar on Trump's record-setting personal earnings.
